title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs Video Lectures (1986)
summary: This is a link to MIT's classic 1986 video lectures for 'Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs' (SICP), a foundational computer science course. The content covers core programming concepts like abstraction, recursion, and interpreters, using Scheme. It is highly influential but predates modern AI/ML, focusing on general programming principles rather than machine learning.
